title: Belgian, French Forces Seize Russian ‘Shadow Fleet’ tanker in North Sea
description: Belgian military forces, supported by the French military, seized a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker Saturday in the North Sea as part of operation “Blue Intruder.” MT Ethera was intercepted in the Belgian Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) while sailing under a false Guinea flag, according to a Belgian Ministry of Defense news release published Sunday. text:
Belgian military forces, supported by the French military, seized a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker Saturday in the North Sea as part of operation “Blue Intruder.”
MT Ethera was intercepted in the Belgian Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) while sailing under a false Guinea flag, according to a Belgian Ministry of Defense news release published Sunday. Ethera was not entitled to free passage through the Belgian EEZ because under international law, it is illegal for a ship to sail under a false flag or without a flag, the release reads. The Belgian government greenlit the boarding of the tanker.
“The ship was sailing under a false flag and is on the European Union’s sanctions list. These are serious indications of deliberate deception and possible sanctions evasion. When such indications arise in our exclusive economic zone, we take action,” Belgium’s Defense Minister Theo Francken said in the release.
The operation began at the Maritime Information Crossroads, an inter-agency center at the Zeebrugge Naval Base that tracks suspicious vessels and analyzes incoming data for navigation behavior, flag, ports visited and any international reports. Following the Belgian government’s approval, the boarding teams approached the ship using NH-90 helicopters, patrol vessels and fast inflatable boats, reads the release.
Patrol vessel BNS Pollux (P902) intercepted Ethera, after which special operations forces operators boarded the tanker. Francken, with Belgian Minister of Justice Annelies Verlinden, watched the operation live as it took place, and posted a video Sunday that shows Belgian special forces personnel rappelling down onto the tanker.
Special forces operators boarded Ethera and secured the bridge, engine room and other areas before signaling that the Federal Public Service Mobility could come aboard to inspect the vessel. Pollux then escorted the suspect vessel to the port of Zeebrugge for further investigation.
Francken in a social media post stated that Ethera would be formally seized at the post. Belgian prosecutors are now questioning Ethera’s captain – a Russian national – and will conduct a criminal investigation.
The French Navy contributed two NH-90s for air support during the operation. The Belgian Air Force also had two NH-90 helicopters participate. French President Emmanuel Macron in a social media post shared Sunday on X said Europe was determined to cut off Russia’s source of funding for its war against Ukraine.
“A major blow to the shadow fleet: in the North Sea, our French Navy helicopters helped last night in the boarding by Belgian forces of an oil tanker under international sanctions. Europeans are determined to cut off the sources of funding for Russia’s war of aggression in Ukraine by enforcing sanctions,” reads Macron’s post.
In January, France seized suspected shadow fleet tanker MT Grinch in the Mediterranean, diverting the ship to the port of Marseille. On Feb. 17, French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot announced on X that the ship was released after paying several million euros in fines.
“Circumventing European sanctions comes at a price. Russia will no longer be able to finance its war with impunity through a shadow fleet off our coast. The oil tanker Grinch will leave French waters after paying several million euros and spending three costly weeks detained in Fos-sur-Mer. Let’s move on,” Barrot said in the X post.
In late January, the 14 coastal states of the Baltic Sea and the North Sea issued a joint statement warning the international maritime community of increasing risks at sea and sounded the alarm on Russia’s shadow fleet activities. The statement was signed by Belgium and France.
“The message is clear: we will take stronger and more coordinated action against vessels suspected of being part of the shadow fleet,” the Belgium Ministry of Defense news release from Sunday concludes.
